samples.xml_description:
Implement a module for parallel-to-serial conversion, where every four input bits are converted to a serial one bit output (from MSB to LSB). The output signal valid_out is set to 1 to indicate the availability of valid serial output. When valid_out = 1, the most significant bit of d is output, and the remaining three bits are output sequentially in the following 3 cycles. Clock signal used for synchronous operations. Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. 4-bit parallel data input. Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output. Serial output representing the converted data. Width of parallel data input. Count of cycles for conversion. Counter register for conversion. Valid signal register for serial output. Data register for parallel data input.

Parallel-to-serial conversion is performed by shifting the data register (data) and outputting the most significant bit (MSB) to the serial output (dout) in a sequential manner. The valid signal (valid) is set to 1 when the conversion is complete and set to 0 during the conversion.

When the counter (cnt) reaches 3, the data register (data) is updated with the parallel data input (d) and the counter (cnt) is reset to 0. Otherwise, the counter (cnt) is incremented by 1 and the data register (data) is shifted left by 1 bit.
